responses.text: The number of suicides among women in Japan increased by almost 15% in 2020.
A total of 440 primary and secondary school students in Japan committed suicide as of the end of November 2020.
Prime Minister Yoshihide Suga has appointed a “Minister for Solitude” to address the worsening suicide problem.
Japan's suicide rate fell 20% in April compared to the same period last year
The suicide rate among women in Japan increased by more than 70% in one month compared to the same period in 2019.
The number of primary and secondary school students who committed suicide in Japan in 2020 was a record high.
Japan is one of the few countries to provide timely information on suicide as it is a major social issue in the island nation.
Sakamoto will coordinate the work of several government agencies that deal with suicide prevention, care for the elderly and children from poor families.

Japan has for years had the highest suicide rate among the G7 industrialized countries.
But despite that, we are seeing a significant increase in the number of suicides.

359 fewer people committed suicide in April compared to the same month last year in Japan.
The coronavirus pandemic has worsened the problems of lonely Japanese residents.
Suicide rates among women increased by 885 to 6,976 in 2020.
In just eight months, more than 300 cases of suicide of children in primary, secondary and high schools were recorded.
Japan’s suicide rate is second only to South Korea
People have been forced to stay at home and face themselves instead of engaging in pastimes and pleasures such as socializing or going out.
The mortality rate of suicide in Japan is higher than in other G7 countries
The biggest drop in five years was possibly due to the coronavirus outbreak.
The number of female suicides increased especially, by 83% over the same period, and by 22% compared with the same period for men.
The number of school-age suicides has more than doubled over the same period of their lives.
Suicides among women in their 20s increased by 17 percent in 2020.
The leading cause of death among young people in Japan is suicide.
Japan is one of the most successful countries in containing the coronavirus outbreak, with a population of more than 126 million people, only about 2,000 victims have been recorded
He may not see any other way out.
Japan will introduce the post of Minister of loneliness in connection with the coronavirus pandemic.
Many faced unemployment, and women had an additional burden at home.
The number of suicides under the age of 20 was 659, an increase of 10.0% year-on-year, the highest since 2000.
The Japanese government pointed out that the number of suicides have increased since July this year.
Japan ranks seventh in the world ranking of sad statistics.
The number of suicides among schoolchildren in Japan was 339 in the previous year.
Japan has one of the highest suicide rates in the world, with 18.5 suicides per 100,000 people
The incidence of coronavirus in Japan is lower than in some other countries, so there are no strict restrictions.
Women are mostly employed in the hotel, catering and retail industries, which have been most affected by the epidemic, and many have been laid off.
75% of Japanese schoolchildren showed signs of stress due to the epidemic, according to an online survey of more than 8,700 parents and children by the Japan Child Health and Development Center.
The limitation of activity and the sense of loss of the meaning of existence have crushed many people with the pain of unseen existence.
In October 2020, 879 women committed suicide, more than 70% higher than the same period in 2019.
The number of suicides among high school students almost doubled compared to the previous year, reaching 138.
The COVID-19 epidemic has affected all aspects of Japanese society.
59 primary and secondary school students committed suicide in August this year, double the number in the same period last year.
The suicide rate among young people in Japan has increased due to bullying in school or other reasons, resulting in excessive pressure on young people.
The epidemic has caused a large number of people to lose their jobs and schools have been closed.
More than 300 primary and secondary school children died within eight months from March to November 2020.

The technology of extracting oxygen from the atmosphere of Mars will help create a breathable atmosphere for astronauts.
“Perseverance” extracted oxygen from the atmosphere of Mars.
Cyanobacteria can use gases in the Martian atmosphere, at low pressure, as a source of carbon and nitrogen.

This will be an important step towards turning Mars into a habitable planet for human colonies.

The atmosphere of Mars consists mainly of carbon dioxide and nitrogen.

Scientists know that in order to live on Mars, even temporarily, astronauts would have to produce some of their needs, especially oxygen for breathing and fuel for travel to Earth or beyond on the Red Planet.
Bayer will pay about $9.5 billion to settle claims filed by lawyers representing some 95,000 plaintiffs.
Bayer is working to resolve about 125,000 similar claims.
The lawsuit was triggered by Bayer’s $63 billion acquisition of Monsanto.
Bayer Group has reached a settlement agreement on its Roundup herbicide carcinogen lawsuit, the amount of the settlement will be between 10.1 billion and 10.9 billion US dollars.
Roundup is Monsanto’s flagship product, with global sales of $4.76 billion in 2015, of which $1.9 billion was for agricultural products, and glyphosate was the largest.
Bayer AG is continuing to work with plaintiffs’ lawyers to develop a joint program to resolve future Roundup-related claims.
